titleStmt.title: Letter from H.S. Hunter to Sir Matthew Nathan, 26 April 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: H.S. Hunter

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from H.S. Hunter to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), the under-secretary for Ireland. Hunter forwards a copy of a letter from the Prisoners' Aid Society, Knockaloe. The letter concerns an internee, Alfred Tonnishoff, who is searching for his leaving certificate from the public school in Hagen, Germany. The certificate was seized when Tonnishoff was arrested on 16 August, 1914, 12 days after Great Britain declared war on Germany. Sir Matthew Nathan was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
49 WELLINGTON STREET, LONDON, W.C. 26th April, 1916. Sir, I am instructed by the Director of the
Prisoners of War Information Bureau to forward herewith,
to be laid before the Lord Lieutenant, the enclosed
copy of a letter from Prisoners' Aid Society at the
Prisoners of War Camp, Knockaloe, which was forwarded
to this Bureau by the American Vice Consul, and to
enquire what reply may be made to the prisoner of war
Alfred TONNISHOFF. I am, Sir,
Your obedient Servant, H.S.Hunter. The Under Secretary,
Dublin Castle,
Dublin,
IRELAND. J.G.
For inquiry
and report
EOF 4/5/16 To Comm Be of not
for report
WM Connell
KNOCKALOE (CAMP 10) PRISONERS' AID SOCIETY Camp 1 Knockaloe, Isle of Man April 6th, 1916. American Consular Service, 45, Broad Street, London, E.C. The here intended p/w No. 14977,
T o e n n i s s h o f f, applied to us and stated;- When I was arrested on August 16th, 1914, at
BELFAST, Ireland by Colonel Hill-Trever and two dectectives,
I was brought to the police station where
all my personal papers were taken from me. Two days
later I was transferred to the detention barracks at
Dublin where all my papers were returned to me but
one:- the leaving certificate of the public school
at H a g e n (Westfalen) (Zeugnis der Reife, Oberrealschule H a g e n, Westfalen). Please try
to recover this certificate which is most valuable to me. In connection with the above statement, we
should be thankful to you if you would kindly take the
necessary steps to recover the certificate for the man. Thanking you in anticipation, we are,
yours obediently
Approved for dispatch:-
(Signed) H. Graham Taylor,
Major.
Assistant Commandant,
for Commandant.
